title: <div> * Dedicated Small Business Field Service Software: (Long-term consideratio
content:
* Dedicated Small Business Field Service Software: (Long-term consideration, but good to know for the future) Solutions like Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceTitan are designed for this and much more, but come with a cost.
Let's detail the Google Forms + Google Sheets approach as it's often a good starting point for your situation:
A. Designing the Google Form (Equipment Request Form):
* Form Name: Majestic Design - Equipment & Supply Request
* Required Fields:
* Employee Name: (Short answer text)
* Date of Request: (Date picker - automatically populated sometimes)
* Job Name/Location: (Dropdown with current active jobs or short answer text) - This is critical!
* Item(s) Needed: (Paragraph text - allow for multiple items and details)
* Example: "100 ft PEX tubing (1/2 inch), 50 qty 1/2 inch copper fittings (elbows), 2 bags PVC glue (small)"
* Urgency: (Multiple choice dropdown: "Standard (Next Day Prep)", "Urgent (Need Today - Describe Why)", "Emergency") - This helps prioritize.
* Reason for Urgency (if Urgent/Emergency): (Paragraph text - conditional on urgency selection)
context: From document: OK, I have been taken in to my father‘s company, Majestic design. It’s… — chunk 16 (chunk 16/22)
